sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 12.84 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.12)

statement.latex: (Well-known problem). Is it true that if there exist two non-iso\-mor\-phic groups with the given set of orders of the elements, then there are infinitely many groups with this set of orders of the elements? { S.\,A.\,Syskin} No, it is not (V.\,D.\,Mazurov, Algebra and Logic, { 33}, no.\,1 (1994), 49--55).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
